sessionCompleted: 🥈 Session complete! You completed {limit} attempts. Take a short break to rest your eyes.
sessionMastered: 🥇 Incredible focus! Your visual cortex has reached its maximum plastic saturation for today. You have successfully mastered Stage 5 at ultra-low contrast! Stop here to lock in these results and let your eyes rest. Outstanding job!
secAboutText: Amblyopia (lazy eye) is not an optical defect of the eye itself—it is a neurological condition of the visual cortex. Over time, the brain's primary visual area (V1) learns to actively suppress (ignore) the signals from the weaker eye. Gabor patches act as the 'letters' of the visual cortex. By forcing your weaker eye to resolve their tilt on the absolute edge of visibility, you trigger neural plasticity, rebuilding and sharpening degraded synaptic connections.
secInstructionsText:
• Application: Use this for 🩹 Classic Occlusion and ⚡ Cortical Speed Blitz protocols.
  • Occlusion: Patch your healthy (strong) eye completely. Only train the amblyopic eye.
  • Distance: Keep your device at arm's length (50–70 cm).
  • Fixation: Fix your gaze strictly on the central cross (+). Press START and determine the Gabor patch tilt.
secLevelsList:
• Use this setup for 🕶️ Binocular Balance, 🎯 Parafoveal Capture, or 🌀 Flicker Resonance protocols.
  • Instructions: Do NOT patch your eye! Wear Red-Cyan 3D glasses (both eyes open).
  • Calibration: Open the Settings and click 'Toggle Fusion Alignment Test' to calibrate. Closing your right eye should reveal only 'L' (Red), closing your left should reveal only 'R' (Cyan).
  • Contrast Balancer: If your strong eye suppresses the lazy eye, lower the 'Strong Eye Contrast Balancer' slider in Settings until the central Gabor patch is clearly visible.
secPresetsText:
• 🩹 Classic Occlusion (Patching): Awaken dormant synapses. Depriving your healthy eye completely forces the visual cortex to allocate 100% of its attentional and metabolic resources to decoding the weaker foveal Gabor signals.
  • ⚡ Cortical Speed Blitz: Train on instant feed-forward impulse. Ultra-short 100ms flashes prevent compensatory eye movements, forcing V1/V2 neurons to decode visual targets instantly.
  • 🕶️ Binocular Balance (3D): Bypassing active suppression. Reducing contrast for the strong eye levels the competitive field under 3D glasses, forcing the brain to accept and integrate inputs from both channels simultaneously.
  • 🌀 Flicker Resonance (3D): Unlocking cortical blockade. Pulsating the target at 10 Hz (the brain's alpha attentional frequency) resonant-activates V1 neurons, helping release chronic dominant-eye suppression.
  • 🎯 Parafoveal Capture (3D): Calibrating the ocular map. Randomly shifting the target to the outer margins trains correct foveal coordinates and counters eccentric fixation.
secRecommendationsText:
• 1. Focus ONLY on the central cross (+): Never chase the Gabor patch with your gaze. Resolving the tilt using your foveal vision trains receptive fields and corrects ocular misalignment.
  • 2. The 15-Minute Rule: Train daily for 100–150 attempts (approx. 15 minutes). Rest is critical for consolidating visual memory; overtraining only causes muscle fatigue.
  • 3. Listen to Your Body: Stop immediately if your eyes water or feel painful. Always prioritize comfort.
descModeExplanation: Preset guidelines: Select an active protocol as a starting template. Modify any checkbox below to seamlessly transition into 'Custom' configuration mode.
helpPresetMode: Clinical pre-sets (active macros) designed for binocular, peripheral, or rapid temporal stimulation.
helpStartLevel: Initial spatial frequency. Higher stages have ultra-fine lines requiring greater visual acuity.
helpFlashDuration: Exposure duration. Short flashes prevent compensatory eye movements (saccades) and force instant processing.
helpStageAdvance: Allows the program to automatically advance or downgrade stages based on your accuracy streak.
helpPeripheral: Randomly shifts the target to train parafoveal spatial localization and peripheral attentional fields.
helpCrowding: Surrounds the target Gabor with side distractors to challenge lateral suppression and the crowding effect.
helpLowContrast: Allows the adaptive contrast staircase to descend down to the extreme 1% threshold instead of 5%.
helpWideVariance: Randomizes the line density, size and aspect ratio (shape) of the Gabor patch with every flash to prevent neural adaptation.
helpStatic: The Gabor patch stays visible indefinitely until you choose an answer (no auto-hiding).
helpFlicker: Pulsates the target at 10 Hz (Alpha-resonance) to bypass chronic cortical suppression of the weak eye.
helpAnaglyph: Splits red/cyan color channels for dichoptic training. Requires Red-Cyan 3D glasses.
helpFusionLock: Renders a zero-disparity frame and corner brackets to stabilize eye alignment and prevent dominant-eye suppression.
helpOrthogonal: Rotates flanking bars perpendicular to the target, creating distinct orientation boundaries.
helpDynamic: Runs a continuous motion wave inside the flankers to saturate dominant eye motion channels.
